full_response:
RAM stands for Random Access Memory. It is a type of computer memory that temporarily stores data and applications while a computer is running. RAM is volatile, meaning its contents are lost when the computer is powered off. RAM is used to improve the performance of a computer by allowing it to access frequently used data and applications quickly. It is an essential component of a computer system, and its capacity can vary greatly depending on the type of computer and its intended use.

What is the difference between RAM and ROM? RAM (Random Access Memory) and ROM (Read-Only Memory) are two types of computer memory.
